business.headline: Thinking of foreclosing your loan? Here's what it does to your credit scoreGot some extra cash and thinking of closing your loan early? It may sound like a smart move, but many borrowers still wonder — what does it do to your credit score?

Will flight ticket prices rise from April?Air travel may get a bit more expensive from April as fuel prices are expected to rise. With airlines facing higher operating costs, will passengers soon have to pay more for tickets?

8th Pay Commission questionnaire deadline today: Have you submitted your response?The clock is ticking for central government employees and stakeholders. With the 8th Pay Commission questionnaire deadline ending today, those who want to share their inputs need to act fast.

Willie Walsh named IndiGo CEO, succeeds Pieter Elbers who quit in MarchIndiGo brings in global aviation veteran Willie Walsh as CEO, replacing Pieter Elbers, as the airline pushes ahead with international expansion and prepares for a more competitive long-haul market strategy.

Your credit report is about to change every week: What borrowers must knowBy the middle of 2026, the era of the monthly credit update will be entirely behind us. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is actively pushing the boundaries of credit reporting, shifting the mandate to ensure that consumer credit data is updated with Credit Information Companies (CICs) on a weekly basis further bettering the existing fortnightly frequency.

Why did Atanu Chakraborty step down from HDFC Bank? Key reasons explainedAtanu Chakraborty resigned as HDFC Bank chairman due to a growing mismatch between his views on ethics and the bank's approach to certain issues, including governance and conduct matters.

Floating vs fixed home loans in 2026: What works better in a volatile market?Borrowers with stable income growth, diversified savings, and the ability to prepay opportunistically may benefit from floating structures. The flexibility to reduce principal during bonus cycles or surplus phases often offsets interim rate volatility.

Final call for taxpayers: 5 easy ways to reduce your tax before March 31 endsWith just hours left before the financial year closes, tax planning becomes urgent. The good news is that some easy investment options can still help you reduce your tax if you act quickly. Let's have a look.

US gasoline crosses $4 a gallon amid Iran war, highest since 2022The US national average retail price of gasoline moved past the $4 mark on Monday. The last time prices were at this level was in August 2022, following Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
Are banks open or closed on Mahavir Jayanti 2026?If you are visiting a bank today for personal work, it is best to check in advance whether your branch is open and what services are available.

IRDAI to introduce simple health covers, rate insurers and hospitals: ReportIRDAI's new steps aim to improve transparency and accountability in the health insurance sector, while addressing long-standing issues such as claim delays, billing disputes, and low insurance penetration.

ITR forms for AY 2026–27 out: Who can file ITR-1 and when should you choose ITR-2?The income tax filing season is here again, and the first step is knowing which form to pick. While ITR-1 works for many, others may need ITR-2, but how do you decide which one applies to you?

US Defense Secretary's broker looked to buy defence fund before Iran attackAccording to the FT report, the investment discussed by Hegseth's broker did not ultimately go ahead as the fund, which launched in May last year, was not yet available for Morgan Stanley clients to buy.

Opening a women-centric bank account? Here's what to actually look forAccording to government data, women today account for nearly 39 percent of bank account holders and contribute close to 40 percent of total deposits in the country.

What is tax-loss harvesting and how can it help you save tax?Tax loss harvesting has become more relevant as stock markets have seen volatility and many investors are sitting on losses in equities. At the same time, assets like gold and silver have delivered strong returns, leading to higher tax liability for some investors.

Is stock market closed or open today? Check NSE, BSE holiday for Mahavir JayantiMarkets will remain shut on March 31 for Mahavir Jayanti and again on April 3 for Good Friday. This means that out of five trading days in the week, markets will be open only for three sessions.

Are buyers choosing paint for identity, not just performance?The paint market is evolving not only in terms of technology but also in consumer expectations. Today's buyers increasingly seek products that reflect their identity and values, rather than just deliver functional outcomes.

From PPF, NSC to SSY: Check latest small savings scheme rates for April–June 2026Looking to invest in small savings schemes this quarter? The latest interest rates for PPF, NSC and SSY are out for April–June 2026, and here's what you need to know.

Oil market well supplied, US to retake control of Hormuz, says Scott BessentUS Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the United States is preparing for a scenario where it would ensure safe passage through the strategic waterway, either through direct naval escorts or a multinational effort.
Made an ITR mistake? File ITR-U before March 31 deadlineMissed reporting some income or spotted an error in your tax return? There's still time to correct it with ITR-U before March 31.

entertainment.headline: Raja Shivaji teaser: Riteish Deshmukh is fierce as Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj The teaser of Raja Shivaji was released on March 31. Riteish Deshmukh plays Maratha warrior king Chhatrapati Shivaji in the upcoming period drama.

Mammootty, Mohanlal's Patriot release postponed, makers to share new date soon The much-awaited Malayalam espionage thriller Patriot, starring Mammootty and Mohanlal, has been postponed due to unforeseen circumstances. The new release date will be announced on April 1, 2026.

Priyanka Chopra Jonas does seva at Golden Temple, cleans utensils. Video Priyanka Chopra Jonas was recently seen performing seva at the Golden Temple in Amritsar, earning praise for staying connected to her roots. Her visit, shortly after attending the Oscars 2026, has sparked speculation about a possible shoot for her upcoming film Varanasi in the city.

Danish Pandor and Aahana Kumra sing Jaiye Sajana from Dhurandhar 2. Watch On a road trip, Danish Pandor and Aahana Kumra were seen singing Jaiye Sajana from Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Danish portrays the character of Uzair Baloch in Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar franchise.

BTS' label launches auditions for an Indian girl band: Are you ready to rock? Auditions for an Indian girl band, being launched by the Korean powerhouse behind K-pop superbands such as BTS, Seventeen and KATSEYE, are on, across multiple cities in India. Read on to know the details.

Where is politics? Danish Pandor hits back amid Dhurandhar 2 propaganda debate Amid the growing debate around Dhurandhar 2, actor Danish Pandor weighed in on the film's "politics" and audience criticism. The actor who plays Uzair Baloch opened up about the film's intense shift, viral scenes, and why he believed it was "just storytelling."

Kalki director Nag Ashwin gives a Dhurandhar 2 shout-out; calls Aditya Dhar genius Praising Dhurandhar: The Revenge, Nag Ashwin said the film goes beyond action to deliver strong emotional depth. He called Aditya Dhar a "genius" and lauded Ranveer Singh's portrayal of Jaskirat Singh Rangi.

Kathanar teaser trailer: Jayasurya-Anushka Shetty's film set for monsoon release The teaser trailer of Kathanar - The Sorcerer starring Jayasurya and Anushka Shetty has been unveiled, hinting at a unique portrayal of Kerala's legendary priest. The film is set for a pan-India release this monsoon season in multiple languages.

Wheel of Fortune: Akshay Kumar admits feeling small around highly educated people Akshay Kumar recently shared that he feels very small in front of highly educated people. The actor advised Wheel of Fortune viewers to value education after sharing his own insecurities.

Dhurandhar 2 becomes first Hindi film to cross Rs 100 crore in Karnataka Dhurandhar 2 has crossed Rs 100 crore in Karnataka, becoming the first Hindi film to achieve the milestone and rewriting box office trends in a traditionally Kannada-dominated market.

Amid Dhurandhar buzz, author Harinder Sikka says trusting Meghna Gulzar was a mistake Author Harinder Sikka openly criticised filmmaker Meghna Gulzar's adaptation of his work amid renewed espionage drama interest.

Syndey Sweeney's The Housemaid's Secret release date announced. Check here The Housemaid's Secret, the sequel to the hit thriller The Housemaid, is set to release in 2027. The sequel stars Sydney Sweeney, Michele Morrone and Kirsten Dunst.

Prakash Raj's mother dies at 86, Pawan Kalyan offers heartfelt condolences Actor Prakash Raj's mother Suvarnalatha died at the age of 86 due to health issues. Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister and actor Pawan Kalyan shared a condolence message on X.

Alka Yagnik opens up about ongoing battle with hearing loss, pauses new projects Alka Yagnik revealed she is still battling a rare hearing disorder more than a year after her diagnosis, keeping her away from new projects.

Ranbir Kapoor explains Lord Ram's legacy, shares 1st glimpse of Ramayana role in LA Ranbir Kapoor unveiled the first glimpse of his character, Lord Rama, from Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana at a special Los Angeles screening. The actor reflected on the legacy of "Maryada Puroshattam" ahead of the film's India reveal on Hanuman Jayanti.

Anupam Kher slams propaganda tag on Dhurandhar 2: People are not idiots Anupam Kher slammed critics calling Dhurandhar 2 propaganda, saying audiences aren't idiots as the Ranveer Singh-starrer sees housefull theatres. Speaking at the International Film Festival Delhi, the veteran actor urged people to ignore irrelevant voices and celebrate the film's success.

Thaai Kizhavi on OTT: When and where to watch Radhikaa Sarathkumar's Tamil drama Thaai Kizhavi, a Tamil rural drama featuring Radikaa Sarathkumar, has been a major hit at the box office and is now set to premiere on JioHotstar. Produced by Sivakarthikeyan Productions, the film's engaging story and strong performances have won positive reviews across India.

Shot Dhurandhar, Ramayana back-to-back with Ranveer, Ranbir: Actor Abhay Arora Actor Abhay Arora opened up about shooting Dhurandhar and Ramayana simultaneously with Ranveer Singh and Ranbir Kapoor. He also addressed propaganda claims around Dhurandhar and shared his experience of working with director Aditya Dhar.

BTS makes waves again as Swim debuts at number 1 on Billboard Hot 100 Global sensation BTS returned with Swim from ARIRANG, which debuted at number 1 on Billboard Hot 100. The single also broke into India's competitive streaming charts, marking a milestone for K-pop in the country.

general news.headline: US won't help you: Trump's oil message to UK, other countries amid Hormuz blockadeAs the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked and allies refuse to join the war, Trump urged nations facing a fuel crunch to fend for themselves as tensions with Iran continue to spiral.

Grabbed breasts amid chants: Stories of godmen abusing women in garb of 'healing'Two women, speaking to India Today Digital, recount their deeply disturbing experiences of sexual abuse at the hands of men who claimed to possess supernatural powers. It was their parents' superstition that led them into the clutches of these godmen and the silence that followed that ultimately shielded the perpetrators. These real stories highlight how silence and superstition empower predatory godmen like Nashik's Ashok Kharat.

Who's losing the war in Iran?Iran doesn't have a lot to lose. The US has a lot: economy, petrodollar, hegemony, and face. Iran is not in a position to lose the war. It's winning. Simply by surviving, for another few weeks.

Oracle is firing 30,000 employees, early morning layoff emails have started arrivingAfter days of speculation, Oracle is beginning layoffs. Reports suggest that around 30,000 employees are expected to be impacted and layoff emails have already started arriving in mailboxes.

From IIT Bombay's 1974 win to Tharoor's podium days: The lost art of campus quizzingA nostalgic look at how quizzes and debates once shaped intellectual life on Indian campuses. A recent post on X brought back memories of that era, with everyone from Jairam Ramesh to Shashi Tharoor sharing their experiences and recalling how, in the days before the internet, these platforms were important sources of knowledge and information.

PBKS vs GT Live: GT (102/2 in 12 ovs). Buttler and Phillips ride their luckPBKS vs GT Live Score: GT (102/2 in 12 ovs). Chahal bowls a tight spell and nearly dismisses Buttler, but GT remain under pressure with their key batter yet to shift gears in the innings.

Smoke alarm pushes Air India Express to land in UP, pilot issues Mayday callAn Air India Express flight from Bagdogra to Delhi made a safe emergency landing in Lucknow with 148 passengers after a mid-air smoke alert.
Come close, we're waiting: Iran dares US as Trump weighs ground op -Iran's Army released a video of elite commandos conducting drills, warning American forces, 'Come close, we are waiting for you'.

Dhurandhar 2's Shirani Sahab dubbed 'Blinkit of Baloch', memes followAccording to meme-makers, Shirani Baloch, who is shown as the leader of the Baloch United Force, is basically the guy who pulls solutions out of thin air with just pure desi jugaad energy.

Live: Mojtaba Khamenei in Iran, avoiding public appearances, says Russian envoyUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: As the Iran-US-Israel conflict intensifies, multiple flashpoints are emerging across the region, signalling a dangerous escalation. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said President Donald Trump is open to asking Middle Eastern allies to help finance ongoing military operations against Iran, even as Tehran insists it will not step back without compensation. Meanwhile, thousands of US Army troops from the elite 82nd Airborne Division have begun arriving in the region, deepening Washington's military footprint.  Iranian sources also claimed a Mahan Air civilian plane bound for New Delhi on a humanitarian medicines mission was struck at Mashhad airport. In Tehran, Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, in a fourth written statement, hailed Revolutionary Guard Navy chief Alireza Tangsiri as a brave symbol of Iran's Gulf presence. He was killed last week in a strike. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest and live updates.

Free LPG cylinders, Rs 2,500 to women: BJP's manifesto ahead of Kerala pollsThe NDA manifesto promises direct cash support, free LPG cylinders and pensions, alongside major infrastructure and governance reforms, as it seeks to position itself as an alternative to LDF and UDF in Kerala.

Video: 2 sisters on way to exam centre fall off moving bus in KarnatakaTwo sisters were injured after falling from an overcrowded KSRTC bus in Dakshina Kannada when its door reportedly opened mid-journey. They were on their way to write their SSLC exam and are now undergoing treatment.

Back to work at 60: What's driving the un-retirement trend?Retirement is no longer the end of the road. Across the US, a growing number of seniors are swapping leisure time for laptops and meetings again. The surprising rise of the Great un-retirement is reshaping how the world views ageing and work.

2 Indians injured after debris from missile interception falls on houses in DubaiThe Dubai Media Office said the debris came from a "successful interception" and landed on houses in southern parts of the city, causing both injuries and property damage. Authorities said emergency teams responded quickly and handled the situation in line with the highest safety standards.

LPG booking leads police to ex-Army captain in wife's murder after 4-year huntFormer Army officer Sandeep Tomar was convicted in 2014 for strangling his wife Shweta and attempting to pass it off as a suicide. He was sentenced to life imprisonment, dismissed from the Army, and jailed in Ferozepur.

Bengaluru techie dies by suicide, wife jumps off 17th floor after finding bodyPreliminary investigation suggested ongoing marital discord behind the couple's decision to take their own lives. Authorities are also probing whether Reddy's health issues played a role in the incident.

How tennis star Leander Paes's BJP entry has a Bengal twistTennis legend Leander Paes embarked on a new political journey by joining the BJP on Tuesday ahead of the Bengal elections. Soon, social media was flooded with questions: Is Leander a Bengali? What is his connection to Bengal?

Make a deal or war will intensify: Hegseth says Iran's new regime should be wiserUS Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth said Iran should agree to a deal to end the month-long war, while adding that the new Iranian regime should be wiser than the previous one.

Gulf turns to a country 4,000 km away to defeat Iran's Shahed dronesSince the war began, the US and its Gulf allies have relied on expensive air defence systems to shoot down cheap Iranian drones. It has saddled them with a bill in tens of billions of dollars. Now, a country that has learnt how to stop these drones in a cheaper, smarter way has come to the rescue.

Ashwini Bhide, close aide of Maharashtra CM, is first woman BMC commissionerKnown as a "no-nonsense" administrator with close ties to Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis, Bhide was selected over several high-ranking contenders like Assem Gupta and Milind Mhaiskar due to her proven track record with mega-projects.

Priyanka Chopra Jonas does seva at Golden Temple, cleans utensils. VideoPriyanka Chopra Jonas was recently seen performing seva at the Golden Temple in Amritsar, earning praise for staying connected to her roots. Her visit, shortly after attending the Oscars 2026, has sparked speculation about a possible shoot for her upcoming film Varanasi in the city.

Trump eyes early Iran war exit. But Arab allies pushing for decisive fire. Why?Five weeks into the war on Iran, US President Donald Trump is reportedly planning an early exit, leaving the Strait of Hormuz choked. However, the US's Gulf allies are pushing it to continue its strikes on Iran and even conduct a ground operation. Why do Saudi Arabia, Kuwait and the UAE want the war to continue?

Cracked such a complex deal for free IPL tickets: Tejasvi Surya jabs Karnataka governmentShivakumar on Monday met with officials of the Karnataka State Cricket Association before announcing that every Karnataka MLA will receive three free tickets to watch IPL matches at the Chinnaswamy Stadium.

The nuclear genie is out of the bottle. A mad rush for nukes has begunThe strikes on Iran are pushing countries in Europe and Asia to rethink their position on nuclear weapons. Nations like Japan, South Korea, Turkey and Saudi Arabia are considering building their own nuclear arsenal. Experts warn of a race for nuclear arms, which could make the world a more dangerous place.

sports.headline: Gukesh opts for workload management, to skip part of Grand Chess tour D Gukesh adjusts his tournament schedule to focus on training and form recovery and has decided to skip part of the Grand Chess tour. Gukesh said he will only participate in two Rapid and Blitz tournaments in Europe.

Will Pant bat at No.3? LSG coach keeps everyone guessing ahead of DC clash Justin Langer keeps Rishabh Pant's batting position under wraps ahead of their IPL 2026 opening match against Delhi Capitals. Pant was rotated around the batting order during the IPL 2025 season, which didn't work out for him.

Who is Ashok Sharma? Uncapped tearaway pacer makes IPL debut for Gujarat Titans Uncapped fast bowler Ashok Sharma, 23, made his IPL debut for Gujarat Titans in their IPL 2026 opener against Punjab Kings in Mullanpur on Tuesday, after being bought for Rs 90 lakh at the Abu Dhabi auction.

Wouldn't have sacked Enzo Maresca as Chelsea coach in January: Marc Cucurella Chelsea defender Marc Cucurella has claimed that he wouldn't have sacked Enzo Maresca mid-season and slammed the club's transfer policy of stacking up on young talents instead of going for established ones.

PBKS vs GT Live: GT (83/2 in 9.3 ovs). Chahal brings Gill's knock to an end PBKS vs GT Live Score: GT (83/2 in 9.3 ovs). Yuzuvendra Chahal shows his class and gets rid of the Gujarat Titans skipper, who has struggled to accelerate the innings after the powerplay.

Fakhar Zaman handed 2-match suspension as ball-tampering row rocks PSL Fakhar Zaman has been suspended for two PSL 2026 matches after being found guilty of ball-tampering during Lahore Qalandars' clash against Karachi Kings, with the PCB ruling that he breached the code for altering the condition of the ball.

RCB calmer than last year, players clear about their roles: Krunal Pandya RCB all-rounder Krunal Pandya believes the atmosphere in the Royal Challengers Bengaluru camp is much calmer than last year, with players having a clearer understanding of their roles this season.

Amelia Kerr continues fairytale run, becomes No.1 Women's T20I all-rounder Amelia Kerr and Georgia Voll scaled new heights in the latest ICC Women's T20I rankings, with the New Zealander becoming the No. 1 all-rounder and the Australian rising to the top spot among batters.

Indian prodigy Atiqa Mir ends WSK run as top female with P8 finish Atiqa Mir wrapped up her WSK Super Masters campaign with a strong P8 finish in a highly competitive field. The 11-year-old also emerged as the top-ranked female driver, underlining her rapid rise in international karting.

Five Red Lights: Suzuka shockwaves raise big questions over F1's new era Suzuka exposed early cracks in Formula 1's new era, from safety concerns to shifting team dynamics. With tensions rising and a break ahead, the season already feels at a defining crossroads.

Test cricket important for other venues too: Ganguly reacts to Kolkata snub Sourav Ganguly backed the expansion of Test cricket to newer venues despite Kolkata missing out on marquee fixtures. He emphasised the need to balance tradition with the broader growth of the format in India.

No T20s for Shreyas Iyer since IPL final: Should Punjab Kings be worried?IPL 2026, PBKS vs GT: Ambati Rayudu has backed Shreyas Iyer to hit the ground running for Punjab Kings despite not playing a T20 since last year's IPL final, saying the experienced skipper should have no trouble settling in.

KKR piling pressure on Cameron Green? Australia coach raises price tag concernsIPL 2026: Australia head coach Andrew McDonald backed Cameron Green amid rising expectations after KKR's opening loss. With a back niggle limiting his bowling, the all-rounder's role remains under focus early in the season.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi backed to break Tendulkar's record with India debut in EnglandMichael Vaughan has backed a bold early India call-up for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after the RR teenager's explosive 15-ball fifty against CSK, saying he should be taken on the white-ball tour of England later this year.

Ravindra Jadeja offers first real measure of the CSK trade narrativeIPL 2026: Ravindra Jadeja offered the first telling response in the RR-CSK trade narrative, using control, variation and awareness to shape the game in Guwahati and underline his enduring T20 value.

Ryan Williams debut hints at new Indian football era despite chaosRyan Williams is set to make his India debut in a dead rubber against Hong Kong. While the campaign has faltered, his arrival hints at a potential shift in India's footballing future.

What makes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stand out? Aakash Chopra explains after CSK blitzIPL 2026, RR vs CSK: Aakash Chopra praised Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's unique bat swing, exceptional wrist work, and explosive power, saying the 15-year-old's technique is unlike anything he has seen before.

Confused Chennai's selection strategy faces scrutiny after Rajasthan horrorIPL 2026, RR vs CSK: Former India spinner Piyush Chawla criticised the five-time champions for looking confused and underprepared during their eight-wicket loss to Rajasthan Royals in Guwahati.

CSK has no place for doubts: Hussey backs players to rise back from RR thrashingIPL 2026: CSK batting coach Mike Hussey backed the team's batting unit despite a heavy loss, urging players to stay clear in their approach. He emphasised that one poor performance should not create doubt early in the season.

Ravindra Jadeja on Shivam Dube wicket celebration: Why not continue it here tooIPL 2026, RR vs CSK: Ravindra Jadeja talked about his celebration after dismissing Shivam Dube in the clash between Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals on Monday in Guwahati.

Noise never gets to me: Riyan Parag shuts RR captaincy critics after CSK winIPL 2026: Riyan Parag shrugged off captaincy criticism after leading RR to a dominant win over CSK. He emphasised that external noise does not affect his approach as he focusses on improving decisions on the field

IPL Play of the Day: Burger too hot for CSK, Archer goes vintage in GuwahatiIPL 2026: Jofra Archer and Nandre Burger swung the game early in Guwahati, exploiting helpful conditions to rip through CSK's top order, setting the tone for a Royals performance built on relentless pressure.

Praggnanandhaa holds joint lead in Candidates after hard-fought draw vs Wei YiR Praggnanandhaa held Wei Yi to a draw to stay joint leader after two rounds of the Candidates. The second round saw a calmer day, with most games ending without a winner.
More chaos in PSL: Shaheen Afridi fined for breaching security protocolLahore Qalandars impose strict fine on Afridi to uphold PSL security standards

Second-season syndrome? Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is a glitch in the matrixRR vs CSK: Conventional wisdom says a sophomore slump is inevitable for young cricketing sensations. But as his latest blitz against Chennai Super Kings in IPL 2026 shows, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is defying that notion.

IPL 2026: Gill, Iyer eye T20 redemption as PBKS test GT in season openerIPL 2026: Shubman Gill and Shreyas Iyer headline a clash where both captains are eager to stamp their authority in T20 cricket. While PBKS will be keen to prove last season was no fluke, GT will look to lean on their overall balance and iron out lingering middle-order concerns.

Paid for T20, got a Test: CSK's flop show in Guwahati triggers memes, refund criesCSK's IPL 2026 opener turned into a nightmare as 127 proved nowhere near enough and Rajasthan Royals romped home in Guwahati. Fans were left fuming, joking they had paid for a Test match.

How is Vaibhav doing this? RR captain amazed by youngster's power-hitting vs CSKRajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag admitted he was left wondering "how is he doing this?" as teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi blazed a breathtaking 15-ball half-century, powering RR to a commanding eight-wicket win over Chennai Super Kings in their IPL 2026 opener in Guwahati on Monday.

Sooryavanshi storm blows in Guwahati, familiar woes haunt new-look ChennaiIPL 2026, RR vs CSK: Rajasthan Royals kickstarted their campaign with a statement win as Vaibhav Sooryavanshi backed up a clinical bowling effort with a ferocious 52 in an eight-wicket thrashing of Chennai Super Kings.

Shubman Gill's clarity marks his evolution as batter and leader for GT: HaydenGujarat Titans batting coach Matthew Hayden has praised captain Shubman Gill for his development both as a batter and a leader. Hayden highlighted Gill's clarity of thought and modern confidence as key factors in his growth at the highest level of cricket.

Ravindra Jadeja gives ex-teammate, Shivam Dube fiery send-off in season openerIPL 2026: Ravindra Jadeja's fiery send-off to Shivam Dube adds edge to IPL 2026 opener as his double strike shifts momentum, while Sanju Samson endures a quiet debut for CSK against RR.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
